general_danger_signs.label: Not able to drink or breastfeed
general_danger_signs.explanation: A child who cannot take any oral fluid is at risk of dehydration and cannot take oral medicines. This sign alone warrants urgent referral.
general_danger_signs.how_to_assess: Ask the mother to offer breast or a cup of clean water. Watch: can the child take the fluid into their mouth and swallow?

general_danger_signs.label: Vomits everything
general_danger_signs.explanation: If a child vomits everything, they cannot retain oral medication or rehydration fluid. Intravenous treatment may be needed.
general_danger_signs.how_to_assess: Ask the mother: has the child vomited everything in the last several hours? A child who keeps down some fluids does not 'vomit everything'.

general_danger_signs.label: Convulsions during this illness
general_danger_signs.explanation: Convulsions may indicate meningitis, cerebral malaria, or other serious conditions requiring urgent hospital care.
general_danger_signs.how_to_assess: Ask the mother: has the child had convulsions (fits) during this illness? Describe: arms and legs stiffen, body shakes, child may lose consciousness.

general_danger_signs.label: Lethargic or unconscious
general_danger_signs.explanation: A lethargic or unconscious child may have meningitis, severe malaria, severe dehydration, or other life-threatening conditions.
general_danger_signs.how_to_assess: A lethargic child is drowsy and does not show interest in surroundings. Cannot be fully awakened. An unconscious child does not respond to voice or touch.
